name: Ram Ramirez
description: Puerto Rican born jazz pianist and composer (1913–1994)
abstract: Roger "Ram" Ramirez (September 15, 1913 – 11 January 1994) was a Puerto Rican jazz pianist and composer. He was a co-composer of the song "Lover Man (Oh, Where Can You Be?)"

quotes.text: embarrassment of the audience. They'd feel, "We shouldn't be here; we should be at Carnegie Hall."
quotes.caption: General comment on audience expectations

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Katz
about_quotes.evidence: one of the five pianists there was Ram Ramirez... So I became friends with him
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Katz interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Flip Phillips
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Frankie Newton in 1939... And those were swinging, like Pete Brown and Frankie, Eddie Doggerty on drums, Harry Prates on the bass and Ram Ramirez on piano.
about_quotes.doc_title: Flip Phillips interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 11, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Burrell
about_quotes.evidence: It was with an organ trio and the organist was Ram Ramirez, the guy who wrote Lover Man.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Burrell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: we got Ram. on piano -- Kirby got Ram Ramirez. He was a fine piano player.

about_quotes.speaker: Hinton Milt
about_quotes.evidence: The mention of Ram Ramirez occurs entirely within the [INTERVIEWER]'s turn ('Ram Ramirez lived over there...'). The [INTERVIEWEE] does not mention Ram Ramirez, nor does he respond to the interviewer's statement about him. Therefore, no personal relationship is established in the interviewee's testim
about_quotes.doc_title: Hinton Milt — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Taylor
about_quotes.evidence: Ram Ramirez comes to mind. Ram was a very fine pianist. He wrote Lover Man... Ram always had a nice job playing solo piano or something while working with a combo but he never really got the opportunity that I think he was ready for at that particular moment in time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Taylor — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
